commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Covered thyself, as if not to see our distress.

commentaries.author: Thomas Aquinas
commentaries.content: So Verse 43 says: "Thou hast wrapped thyself with anger and persued us." That is, first by diverse tribulations. And finally: "slaying without pity". For, Proverbs 6:34 claims: "For jealousy makes a man furious, and he will not spare when he takes revenge."

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
